text: This paper examines the formal and technical conditions for utilizing calcareous fly ash as an active pozzolanic-hydraulic component in cements and various binding agents. The study is supported by experimental data obtained from hydraulic road binders and masonry cements formulated with calcareous fly ash sourced from the Bełchatów Power Station. To provide a comprehensive assessment, the researchers evaluated binders with varying proportions of calcareous fly ash, as well as blended systems incorporating ashes from different combustion technologies, including siliceous and fluidized-bed combustion fly ashes. The scope of the investigation focused on key normative properties required for certification and practical application. The findings demonstrate that calcareous fly ash from the Bełchatów Power Station is a highly effective constituent for producing binders and cements that fully comply with current engineering standards and technical approvals.
